mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns because consumer demand flows directly into corporate revenue expectations and top-line earnings revisions.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes show Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off repositioning and downward earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) generates negative real interest rates that inflate equity risk premiums and drive capital into stocks, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in rolling 3-year regressions, or 60-day forward returns are not higher when both conditions hold jointly versus independently.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is held below 1%, positive surprises in industrial production (INDPRO) signal escaping velocity from the zero lower bound and accelerating output, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances, or the conditional return spread versus high-rate periods is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% forecasts contraction in construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than half of the triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) over a two-month span signals capacity utilization improvement and operating leverage-driven earnings rebound, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO two-month acceleration produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll gains (PAYEMS) above 150 thousand per month confirm wage-income growth that supports consumption and corporate pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS increases above 150k coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 2%, month-over-month CPI acceleration (CPIAUCSL) forces discount rate expectations higher and compresses equity multiples, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%, CPIAUCSL acceleration is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in rolling 2-year regressions.
